intelligence.hypothesis: Insufficient telemetry; awaiting next valid capture.

vision_report.compositional_truth_check: The biome is severely depleted. P1 (Yellow Pot) is empty/soil only. P2/P4 (Black Pot) shows no viable plant matter. P3 (Black Pot) contains the white rabbit anchor but no visible Pothos foliage.

plant_audit.P1: Empty soil surface; no signs of String of Nickels.
plant_audit.P2: No evidence of Mexican Mint; soil appears undisturbed.
plant_audit.P3: White rabbit anchor present; Pothos specimen is absent.
plant_audit.P4: No evidence of Silver Guest; shared pot with P2 is barren.

vision_report.biome_observations: Soil appears dark and potentially hydrophobic in the yellow pot. No incidental growth or moss detected. Desk surface is clear of debris.
vision_report.temporal_deltas: Earliest image shows a single green sprout/stem fragment in the P3/P4 area. T-1 shows extreme low-light conditions with total obscuration of biological matter. Current image confirms the loss of the previously observed sprout; the biome is now entirely dormant or deceased.
vision_report.visual_health_inference: Total biome failure. The absence of chlorophyll-bearing structures across all pots indicates a terminal state for the registered specimens.
vision_report.anomalies: None. The primary anomaly is the complete disappearance of the previously identified sprout.
vision_report.narrative_description: I have performed a sequential audit of the provided images. My first action was to establish the spatial orientation of the pots relative to the white rabbit anchor. Upon validation, I compared the earliest image—which contained a single, fragile green sprout—against the current state. The sprout has vanished, and no other plant life is visible. The biome has transitioned from a state of 'critical struggle' to 'total systemic loss'.
